frameworks.name: decoded fMRI neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: MVPA-driven reward of multivariate brain patterns to induce specific neural states

frameworks.name: frontoparietal network theory of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: frontoparietal high-confidence patterns proposed as gating mechanism for awareness

frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: voxel-pattern decoding of confidence and color used as neurofeedback target

frameworks.name: prestimulus spontaneous activity accounts of perception
frameworks.one_line: trial-by-trial fluctuations in baseline activity predict perceptual reports

core_question: Can spontaneous neural activity be made consciously perceived by co-inducing visual and frontoparietal patterns via decoded neurofeedback?
key_assumptions: conscious perception requires conjoint visual and frontoparietal pattern activation
MVPA-decoded patterns causally manipulable through reward feedback
prestimulus activity alone is insufficient for awareness in forced-choice tasks
manipulations: rewarded simultaneous induction of frontoparietal high-confidence patterns and visual color patterns
presence vs. absence of veridical color stimuli during induced states
key_findings: false color perception co-occurred with activation of frontoparietal high-confidence patterns
false color perception was not associated with activation of color-specific patterns in visual cortex
interpretations: frontoparietal networks may uniquely promote spontaneous representations from unconscious to conscious states
visual cortex pattern activation is not sufficient on its own for illusory color awareness
